zh.lifeSummary: 當Nicholas Marshall於1750年出生在美國賓夕法尼亞州亞當斯劍橋大學默里 奧可福德時，他的父親Francis Nicholas Marshall22歲，母親Marguerite Grandadam21歲。他和Mary Caterine至少有1個兒子和1個女兒。他在美國賓夕法尼亞州亞當斯貝維其可 堂史普住了大約20年。他於1812年登記服兵役。他去世於1845年在美國俄亥俄州可倫比納亞里斯本享年95歲。

zh-hans.lifeSummary: 当Nicholas Marshall于1750年出生在美国宾夕法尼亚州亞當斯劍橋大學默里 奧可福德时，他的父亲Francis Nicholas Marshall22岁，母亲Marguerite Grandadam21岁。他和Mary Caterine至少有1个儿子和1个女儿。他在美国宾夕法尼亚州亞當斯貝維其可 堂史普住了大约20年。他于1812年登记服兵役。他去世于1845年在美国俄亥俄州可倫比納亞里斯本享年95岁。

nameMeanings.name: Marshall
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: English: usually an occupational name ‘farrier’, occasionally a status name ‘chief official of a royal household or court; a high officer of state’, from Middle English mareshal and Old French maresc(h)al. An even wider range of meanings is found in some other languages: compare for example Polish Marszałek (see Marszalek ). This name has been established in Ireland since the 13th century. It is also borne by Jews, presumably as an Americanized form of one or more similar (like-sounding) Jewish surnames.
Americanized form of German   Marschall  .
Americanized form of French   Mercier  .

variants.name: Marchel
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Polish Jacek, Ryszard.  German Ernst.

Polish: probably from French maréchal or Old French maresc(h)al, which was originally an occupational name for someone who was in charge of the horses of a household, later a military rank for a senior general. See Marshall and compare Marszalek .

variants.name: Mercier
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  French Armand, Marcel, Andre, Pierre, Gaston, Lucien, Aurele, Jacques, Normand, Laurent, Michel, Valmont.

French and Channel Islands (Jersey): occupational name for a merchant, from Old French mercier, from Late Latin merciarius (an agent derivative of merx, genitive mercis ‘merchandise’). This name was also taken to England by Huguenot refugees. Compare Marshall and Mercy .

variants.name: Maniscalco
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Italian Salvatore, Vito, Sal, Antonio, Ignazio, Carmelo, Francesco, Gaspare, Saverio, Antonino, Carlo, Damiano.

Italian (southern): occupational name from maniscalco ‘farrier’, altered from marescalco by folk etymology, probably influenced by mano ‘hand’, from medieval Latin mariscalcus, marescalcus. Compare Marshall .

variants.name: Marchal
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  French Gilles, Jacques, Maryse, Philippe, Pierre, Veronique.

French and Walloon: status name or occupational name from Old French maresc(h)al ‘marshal’. The term is of ancient Germanic origin (from marah ‘horse, mare’ + scalc ‘servant’) and was originally applied to a man who looked after horses. In the Middle Ages it also came to be used on the one hand as an occupational name for a farrier, and on the other as a status name for an officer of state, in particular a member of a royal household with military responsibilities. Compare English Marshall and 2 below.
English: variant of   Marshall  . This form of the surname is rare in Britain and Ireland, and is found mainly in Glamorgan, Wales.

variants.name: Maskell
meanings: English: variant of Marshall , derived from Middle English, Old French maresc(h)al ‘marshal’. The first element in the placename Mascallsbury (Essex) derives from the surname of a 13th-century individual named Robert le Marescal.

variants.name: Marszalek
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Polish Piotr, Alicja, Andrzej, Eugeniusz, Henryk, Jerzy, Kazimierz, Mieczyslaw, Wieslaw, Zbigniew, Zofia, Zygmunt.

Polish (Marszałek): occupational or status name from the vocabulary word marszałek ‘marshal’, denoting anything from a high court official, a field marshal, or the chairman of the Polish parliament, to the senior servant in a great household. Compare English Marshall .

variants.name: Marshell
meanings: English: variant of   Marshall  .

variants.name: Marschall
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  German Kurt, Erwin, Hedwig, Juergen, Reinhard, Theresia, Wilfried.

German: occupational name for a man in the service of a member of the nobility, originally one who looked after the horses, Middle High German marschalc (from mar(c) ‘noble horse’ + schalk ‘servant’). In time the term came to be used for a military rank or a certain court official. Compare Marshall .
English: variant of   Marshall  . This form of the surname is rare in Britain and Ireland.

variants.name: Marshal
meanings: English and Scottish: variant of   Marshall  .

nameMeanings.name: Nicholas
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: form of the post-classical Greek personal name Nikolaos, derived from nikē ‘victory’ + laos ‘people’. The spelling with -ch- first occurred as early as the 12th century, and became firmly established at the time of the Reformation, although Nicolas is still occasionally found. St Nicholas was a 4th-century bishop of Myra in Lycia, about whom virtually nothing factual is known, although a vast body of legend grew up around him, and he became the patron saint of Greece and of Russia, as well as of children, sailors, merchants, and pawnbrokers. His feast day is 6 December , and among the many roles which legend has assigned to him is that of bringer of Christmas presents, in the guise of ‘Santa Claus’ (an alteration of the Dutch form of his name, Sinterklaas).

variants.name: Nickolas
meanings: Mainly U.S.: variant spelling of   Nicholas  , influenced by the short form   Nick .

variants.name: Nicolas
meanings: From Late Greek. See Nicholas .

historicEvents.description: Thomas Jefferson's American Declaration of Independence endorsed by Congress. Colonies declare independence.
The Declaration of Independence was signed in Philadelphia on July 4, 1776. The liberty bell was first rung here to Celebrate this important document.

historicEvents.title: Movement to Washington D.C.
historicEvents.description: While the growth of the new nation was exponential, the United States didn’t have permanent location to house the Government. The First capital was temporary in New York City but by the second term of George Washington the Capital moved to Philadelphia for the following 10 years. Ultimately during the Presidency of John Adams, the Capital found a permanent home in the District of Columbia.
Ohio was the first state admitted to the Union from the Northwest Territory.

historicEvents.title: The Crimes Act
historicEvents.description: The Crimes Act was made to provide a clearer punishment of certain crimes against the United States. Part of it includes: Changing the maximum sentence of imprisonment to be increased from seven to ten years and changing the maximum fine from $5,000 to $10,000.

historicEvents.title: Kirtland Temple Dedicated
historicEvents.description: On March 27, 1836, the Kirtland Temple was dedicated.

en.lifeSummary: When Nicholas Marshall was born in 1750, in New Oxford, Adams, Pennsylvania, United States, his father, Francis Nicholas Marshall, was 22 and his mother, Marguerite Grandadam, was 21. He had at least 1 son and 1 daughter with Mary Caterine. He lived in Berwick Township, Adams, Pennsylvania, United States for about 20 years. He registered for military service in 1812. He died in 1845, in Lisbon, Columbiana, Ohio, United States, at the age of 95.

fr.lifeSummary: Lorsque Nicholas Marshall est né en 1750, à New Oxford, Adams, Pennsylvanie, États-Unis, son père, Francis Nicholas Marshall, avait 22 ans et sa mère, Marguerite Grandadam, avait 21 ans. Il a eu au moins 1 fils et 1 fille avec Mary Caterine. Il a vécu à Berwick Township, Adams, Pennsylvanie, États-Unis pendant environ 20 ans. Il s'est inscrite au service militaire en 1812. Il est décédé en 1845, à Lisbon, Columbiana, Ohio, États-Unis, à l’âge de 95 ans.

de.lifeSummary: Als Nicholas Marshall 1750 in New Oxford, Adams, Pennsylvania, Vereinigte Staaten geboren wurde, war sein Vater Francis Nicholas Marshall 22 und seine Mutter Marguerite Grandadam 21. Er hatte mit Mary Caterine mindestens 1 Sohn und 1 Tochter. Er hat ungefähr 20 Jahre lang in Berwick Township, Adams, Pennsylvania, Vereinigte Staaten gelebt. Er hat sich 1812 zum Militärdienst angemeldet. Er starb 1845 im Alter von 95 Jahren in Lisbon, Columbiana, Ohio, Vereinigte Staaten.

it.lifeSummary: Quando Nicholas Marshall è nato il nel 1750 nel New Oxford, Adams, Pennsylvania, Stati Uniti d'America, il suo padre, Francis Nicholas Marshall, aveva 22 anni e il suo madre, Marguerite Grandadam, aveva 21 anni. Lui avuto almeno 1 figlio e 1 figlia con Mary Caterine. Vissuto a Berwick Township, Adams, Pennsylvania, Stati Uniti d'America per circa 20 anni. Si è arruolato nell'esercito nel 1812. Morì nel 1845, nel Lisbon, Columbiana, Ohio, Stati Uniti d'America, all'età di 95 anni.

ja.lifeSummary: Nicholas Marshallが1750年にアメリカ合衆国ペンシルベニア州アダムズニュー オクスフォードで生まれた時，父Francis Nicholas Marshallは22歳，母Marguerite Grandadamは21歳であった。彼はMary Caterineとの間に少なくとも1人の息子と1人の娘をもうけた。彼はアメリカ合衆国ペンシルベニア州アダムズバーウィック トウンシュイップに約20年間住んでいた。彼は1812年に兵役登録をした。彼は1845年にアメリカ合衆国オハイオ州コルンビアナリスボンで95歳で死去した。

ko.lifeSummary: Nicholas Marshall(이)가 1750년 미국 펜실베이니아 주 애댐스 뉴 스퍼드에서 태어났을 때, 그의 아버지 Francis Nicholas Marshall(은)는 22세였고, 그의 어머니 Marguerite Grandadam(은)는 21세였다. 그는 Mary Caterine(와)과 적어도 한 명의 아들과 한 명의 딸을 두었다. 미국 펜실베이니아 주 애댐스 버윅 타운시프에서 약 20년 동안 살았다. 그는 1812년에 군복무를 위해 등록했다. 그는 1845년 미국 오하이오 주 콜럼비아나 리스본에서 95세의 나이로 사망했다.

pt.lifeSummary: Quando Nicholas Marshall nasceu em 1750, em New Oxford, Adams, Pensilvânia, Estados Unidos da América, seu pai, Francis Nicholas Marshall, tinha 22 anos e sua mãe, Marguerite Grandadam, tinha 21 anos. Ele teve pelo menos 1 filho e 1 filha com Mary Caterine. Ele viveu em Berwick Township, Adams, Pensilvânia, Estados Unidos da América por quase 20 anos. Ele alistou-se no serviço militar em 1812. Ele faleceu em 1845, em Lisbon, Columbiana, Ohio, Estados Unidos da América, com 95 anos.

ru.lifeSummary: Когда Nicholas Marshall родился в 1750 г. в местности ню оксфорд, адамс, Пенсильвания, Соединенные штаты, его отцу по имени Francis Nicholas Marshall было 22 года, а матери, которую звали Marguerite Grandadam, 21. У него был по крайней мере 1 сын и 1 дочь от Mary Caterine. Он жил в местности бирвикк тоуншип, адамс, Пенсильвания, Соединенные штаты около 20 лет. В 1812 году он поступил на военную службу. Он умер в 1845 г. в местности лисбон, колумбяна, Огайо, Соединенные штаты в возрасте 95 лет.

es.lifeSummary: Cuando Nicholas Marshall nació en 1750, en New Oxford, Adams, Pensilvania, Estados Unidos, su papá, Francis Nicholas Marshall, tenía 22 años y su mamá, Marguerite Grandadam, tenía 21 años. Tuvo por lo menos 1 hijo y 1 hija con Mary Caterine. Vivió en Berwick Township, Adams, Pensilvania, Estados Unidos durante unos 20 años. Se enlistó en el ejército en 1812. Murió en 1845, en Lisbon, Columbiana, Ohio, Estados Unidos, a la edad de 95 años.
